meaning: adj.阴森的,昏暗的,阴天的,忧郁的
examples: There were the two somber figures still following him, though their black sacks were drenched and dripping with water.
Prime Minister Tony Blair was somber.
In fact, he sounded somber and contrite.
He at least made the right choice for someone who could fire up a somber crowd.
In a brief, somber finale, all look forward to a brighter, more peaceful time ahead.
